revision.comment: 新しいページ：太子西山古墳（大阪府太子町にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 太子西山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
前方部正面
（[[敏達天皇]]河内磯長中尾陵・[[石姫皇女]]磯長原陵 拝所） |別名 = 奥城古墳 |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字太子 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 42.60 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 7.75 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長113m（93m） |築造年代 = [[古墳時代]]後期前半 |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第30代[[敏達天皇]]・[[石姫皇女]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |史跡指定 = [[宮内庁]]治定「河内磯長中尾陵」・「磯長原陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 太子西山
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''太子西山古墳'''（たいしにしやまこふん、奥城古墳<おくつきこふん>{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]太子にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長中尾陵'''（こうちのしながのなかのおのみささぎ）」および「'''磯長原陵'''（しながのはらのみささぎ）」として第30代[[敏達天皇]]・[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]][[皇后]]）の[[陵墓|合葬陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、葉室集落西方を南北に伸びる丘陵の鞍部に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。磯長谷に分布する[[磯長谷古墳群]]のうちでは唯一の前方後円墳になる。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北西方に向ける{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。墳丘周辺では[[埴輪]]片が採集されているほか、墳丘周囲には空壕が巡らされる{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[横穴式石室]]の使用が推測される。 この太子西山古墳は、出土埴輪等より[[古墳時代]]後期前半頃の築造と推定される。現在は[[宮内庁]]により第30代[[敏達天皇]]（[[585年]]?崩御）および母の[[石姫皇女]]（崩御年不詳）の墓に治定されているが、被葬者は必ずしも詳らかでない{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。磯長谷では敏達天皇陵のほか[[用明天皇|用明]]・[[推古天皇|推古]]・[[孝徳天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。 * 墳丘長：113メートル（または93メートル[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html 敏達天皇陵（太子西山古墳/奥城古墳）]（太子町ホームページ）。） * 後円部 直径：58メートル * 前方部 幅：67メートル == 被葬者 == 太子西山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第30代'''[[敏達天皇]]'''および母の'''[[石姫皇女]]'''（いしひめのひめみこ）の合葬陵に治定している[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。敏達天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では敏達天皇14年（[[585年]]?）『日本書紀』敏達天皇14年（585年?）8月己亥（15日）条。に崩御したとし、[[崇峻天皇]]4年（[[591年]]?）『日本書紀』崇峻天皇4年（591年?）4月甲子（12日）条。には先に亡くなっていた石姫皇女（没年不詳）の墓である「磯長陵」に追葬されたとする{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では敏達天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長中尾陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとするほか、石姫皇女墓が遠墓の「磯長原墓」として記載され、河内国石川郡敏達天皇陵内の所在で、守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。その後、元禄の探陵の時点では[[叡福寺]]領であったほか、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）には拝所設置、[[1899年]]（明治32年）には御在所修補がなされている{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が敏達天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最後の前方後円墳に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}、埴輪列を有する古墳としても最後の陵に位置づけられる{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。また敏達天皇・石姫皇女が別々に記載されることから、後円部・前方部での別埋葬を推測する説もある{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。一方で円筒埴輪の存在から、時代的に本古墳の敏達天皇陵への比定には否定的な説もある{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[関晃]] 「敏達天皇」|ref={{Harvid|敏達天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「河内磯長中尾陵」（敏達天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=太子西山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=敏達天皇陵|title=王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群 -太子町の古墳墓-|publisher=太子町教育委員会（太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館）|pages=14-15|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[敏達天皇]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html 河内磯長中尾陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html 敏達天皇陵（太子西山古墳/奥城古墳）] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たいしにしやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

（[[敏達天皇]]河内磯長中尾陵・[[石姫皇女]]磯長原陵 拝所） |別名 = 奥城古墳 |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]大字太子 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 42.60 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 7.75 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長113m（93m） |築造年代 = [[古墳時代]]後期前半 |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第30代[[敏達天皇]]・[[石姫皇女]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |史跡指定 = [[宮内庁]]治定「河内磯長中尾陵」・「磯長原陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 太子西山
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''太子西山古墳'''（たいしにしやまこふん、奥城古墳<おくつきこふん>{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]太子にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内磯長中尾陵'''（こうちのしながのなかのおのみささぎ）」および「'''磯長原陵'''（しながのはらのみささぎ）」として第30代[[敏達天皇]]・[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]][[皇后]]）の[[陵墓|合葬陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、葉室集落西方を南北に伸びる丘陵の鞍部に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。磯長谷に分布する[[磯長谷古墳群]]のうちでは唯一の前方後円墳になる。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北西方に向ける{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。墳丘周辺では[[埴輪]]片が採集されているほか、墳丘周囲には空壕が巡らされる{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[横穴式石室]]の使用が推測される。 この太子西山古墳は、出土埴輪等より[[古墳時代]]後期前半頃の築造と推定される。現在は[[宮内庁]]により第30代[[敏達天皇]]（[[585年]]?崩御）および母の[[石姫皇女]]（崩御年不詳）の墓に治定されているが、被葬者は必ずしも詳らかでない{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。磯長谷では敏達天皇陵のほか[[用明天皇|用明]]・[[推古天皇|推古]]・[[孝徳天皇]]陵と[[聖徳太子]]墓が伝わっており、これらは「梅鉢御陵」と総称される。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。 * 墳丘長：113メートル（または93メートル[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html 敏達天皇陵（太子西山古墳/奥城古墳）]（太子町ホームページ）。） * 後円部 直径：58メートル * 前方部 幅：67メートル == 被葬者 == 太子西山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第30代'''[[敏達天皇]]'''および母の'''[[石姫皇女]]'''（いしひめのひめみこ/いしひめのおうじょ）の合葬陵に治定している[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|磯長原陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。敏達天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では敏達天皇14年（[[585年]]?）『日本書紀』敏達天皇14年（585年?）8月己亥（15日）条。に崩御したとするほか、その後の[[殯]]を経て、[[崇峻天皇]]4年（[[591年]]?）『日本書紀』崇峻天皇4年（591年?）4月甲子（12日）条。には先に亡くなっていた石姫皇女（崩御年不詳）の墓に追葬されたとし、その陵号を「磯長陵」とする{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では敏達天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長中尾陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとするほか、石姫皇女墓は遠墓の「磯長原墓」として記載され、河内国石川郡敏達天皇陵内の所在で、守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。その後、元禄の探陵の時点では[[叡福寺]]領であったほか、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）には拝所設置、[[1899年]]（明治32年）には御在所修補がなされている{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が敏達天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最後の前方後円墳に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}、埴輪列を有する陵としても最後に位置づけられる{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。また文献では敏達天皇陵・石姫皇女墓が別々に記載されることから、後円部・前方部での別埋葬と推測する説もある{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。一方で円筒埴輪の存在から、時代的に本古墳の敏達天皇陵への比定には否定的な説もある{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}。 {{Supra|37}} [[斉明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|斉明天皇}}]]|03={{Supra|34}} [[舒明天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}} |} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[関晃]] 「敏達天皇」|ref={{Harvid|敏達天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「河内磯長中尾陵」（敏達天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=[[川副武胤]] 「石姫皇女」|ref={{Harvid|石姫皇女（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「磯長原陵」（石姫皇女項目内）|ref={{Harvid|磯長原陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=太子西山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=敏達天皇陵|title=王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群 -太子町の古墳墓-|publisher=太子町教育委員会（太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館）|pages=14-15|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[敏達天皇]] * [[石姫皇女]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html 河内磯長中尾陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html 敏達天皇陵（太子西山古墳/奥城古墳）] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たいしにしやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

太子西山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第30代'''[[敏達天皇]]'''および母の'''[[石姫皇女]]'''（いしひめのひめみこ/いしひめのおうじょ）の合葬陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）11コマ。{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|磯長原陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 407。。敏達天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では敏達天皇14年（[[585年]]?）『日本書紀』敏達天皇14年（585年?）8月己亥（15日）条。に崩御したとするほか、その後の[[殯]]を経て、[[崇峻天皇]]4年（[[591年]]?）『日本書紀』崇峻天皇4年（591年?）4月甲子（12日）条。には先に亡くなっていた石姫皇女（崩御年不詳）の墓に追葬されたとし、その陵号を「磯長陵」とする{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。また『[[古事記]]』では「御陵在川内科長」と見える。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では敏達天皇陵は遠陵の「河内磯長中尾陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[石川郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとするほか、石姫皇女墓は遠墓の「磯長原墓」として記載され、河内国石川郡敏達天皇陵内の所在で、守戸3烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。その後、元禄の探陵の時点では[[叡福寺]]領であったほか、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）には拝所設置、[[1899年]]（明治32年）には御在所修補がなされている{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。 本古墳が敏達天皇陵であれば、天皇陵としては最後の前方後円墳に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=14-15}}、埴輪列を有する陵としても最後に位置づけられる{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。また文献では敏達天皇陵・石姫皇女墓が別々に記載されることから、後円部・前方部での別埋葬と推測する説もある{{Sfn|河内磯長中尾陵（国史）}}。一方で円筒埴輪の存在から、時代的に本古墳の敏達天皇陵への比定には否定的な説もあり{{Sfn|太子西山古墳（平凡社）|1986年}}{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=14-15}}、真陵の所在に関しては[[葉室塚古墳]]（太子町葉室）に比定する説も挙げられている{{Sfn|王陵の谷・磯長谷古墳群|1994年|pp=38-41}}。 revision.text:
{{Ancient Tomb of Japan |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishihime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mt. Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * **Mound length**: 113 m (or 93 m) * **Rear round section**: - Diameter: 58 m * **Front square section**: - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Front part of the mound

However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * **Mound length**: 113 m (or 93 m) * **Rear round section**: - Diameter: 58 m * **Front square section**: - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishihime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

{{Ancient Tomb of Japan |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishihime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

#REDIRECT [[Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]]
[[Category:mass bump]]

{{Ancient Tomb of Japan |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishihime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishihime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishihime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishihime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishihime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]]

Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka
