{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': - Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': - Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |shape = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{Infobox ancient site |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb worship site)}} |alias = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude = 34|30|42.60 |longitude = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |construction_period = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial_facility = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] designated) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |artifacts = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Kofun |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |label_position = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. It is the only keyhole-shaped tumulus within this cluster. As it is designated an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == Dimensions == The mound dimensions are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last keyhole-shaped imperial tomb and the final tumulus featuring haniwa{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi
