Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] other_designation1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is other_designation1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] other_designation1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is other_designation1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}}

If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}}

Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}}

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}}

Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{citation needed|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Front part of the mound
{{small|([[Emperor Bidatsu]]'s Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb and [[Princess Ishi-hime]]'s Shinaga Plain Tomb {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}})}} |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|30|42.60 |coordinates = 135|38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a [[Horizontal stone chamber]] |occupants = ([[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime]] |burial goods = [[Haniwa]] fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a [[Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (Empress of [[Emperor Kinmei]]). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large [[Zenpokoenfun]] located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. It is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the [[Imperial Household Agency]], and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, [[Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=draft}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Imperial Tombs|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last [[Zenpokoenfun]] imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring [[haniwa]]{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{citation needed|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]
