It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is the only {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Mausoleums|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太子西山古墳|version=93243016|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751982}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太子西山古墳|version=93243016|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751982}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:太子西山古墳]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|WD=Q1347173}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.511833|N|135.635486|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|30|42.60 |38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |occupants = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}} |part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11588120}} }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is the only {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Mausoleums|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太子西山古墳|version=93243016|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751982}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

While the actual occupants are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is the only {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Mausoleums|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太子西山古墳|version=93243016|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751982}} [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Mausoleums|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太子西山古墳|version=93243016|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751982}} [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]

revision.text:
[[ja:太子西山古墳]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} |name = Taishi Nishiyama Kofun |image = File:Taishi Nishiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg |alternate_name = Okutsuki Kofun |group = {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi|WD=Q1347173|1=Taishi, Osaka}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.511833|N|135.635486|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|30|42.60 |38|7.75 |iso_code = JP-27 |type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] |size = Mound length: 113 m (or 93 m) |built = Early Late [[Kofun period]] |burial = Presumed to be a {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |occupants = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site1) [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments |imperial_tomb = {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |special_notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Taishi Nishiyama
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - Northwest |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} |part_of = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11588120|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster}} }} '''Taishi Nishiyama Kofun''' (太子西山古墳), also known as '''Okutsuki Kofun''' (奥城古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. While the actual occupants are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint burial tomb for [[Emperor Bidatsu]] and {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}). == Overview == Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located on the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture. It was built at the saddle of a ridge extending north to south, west of the Hamuro settlement in Isonokami Valley, typeing part of the {{ill|Isonokami Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is the only {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} within this cluster. As it is Historic Site1 an imperial tomb, it is under the administration of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, and no typeal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus faces northwest, featuring a two-tiered mound construction{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html|title=Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)|publisher=Taishi Town Official Website}}. Haniwa fragments have been collected from the mound, and there is evidence of a surrounding moat{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. The burial facility is presumed to be a horizontal stone chamber. Based on haniwa and other burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early part of the late [[Kofun period]]. == area == The mound area are as follows{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986|pages= }}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}: * '''Mound length''': 113 m (or 93 m) * '''Rear round section''': ** Diameter: 58 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 67 m == Occupants == The actual occupants of Taishi Nishiyama Kofun remain unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint tomb of the 30th [[Emperor Bidatsu]] (崩御 in 585?) and his mother, {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (year of death unknown). The tomb is referred to as the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|lt=Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja|河内磯長中尾陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太子西山古墳}} and {{ill|Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja|磯長原陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga Plain Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html|title=List of Mausoleums|publisher=Imperial Household Agency}}. If this tumulus is indeed Emperor Bidatsu's tomb, it would be the last {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} imperial tomb and the final [[kofun]] featuring {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=14–15}}. However, the presence of cylindrical haniwa casts doubt on its identification with Emperor Bidatsu's tomb{{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|author=Heibonsha|year=1986}}. Some researchers suggest the actual tomb might be {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}}, also located in Taishi Town{{Cite book|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|author=Taishi Town Board of Education|year=1994|pages=38–41}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Osaka Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1994|title=Isonokami Kofun Cluster - The Royal Tombs of Taishi Town|publisher=Taishi Town Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/030/index.html Kawachi Shinaga Naka no O Tomb] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/bidatsutennoryo.html Emperor Bidatsu's Tomb (Taishi Nishiyama Kofun)] - Taishi Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|太子西山古墳|version=93243016|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:太子西山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751982}} [[Category:Emperor Bidatsu]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
